skill.name: pdf-page-extract
skill.description: Extract PDF page text, layout metadata, rendered PNGs, embedded images, and page mappings into persistent artifacts for downstream processing.
skill.summary: Create traceable PDF page extraction artifacts for layout-aware document processing.
skill.author: AbeJitsu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command and filesystem patterns in SKILL.md. Review confirms the command execution and file writes are intentional PDF extraction workflow steps, while the path traversal and blocker findings are false positives from documented relative paths and ordinary text.

medium_findings.title: Local Python Command Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run local Python scripts and inline Python for PDF page mapping, rich extraction, rendering, and image extraction. This is expected for the workflow, but users should run it only in trusted repositories because those commands execute local code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands are explicit and central to the skill behavior. They appear legitimate, but executing local repository scripts is still a real operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Reads and Persistent Artifact Writes
medium_findings.description: The skill reads PDFs and writes JSON and PNG artifacts under analysis and output directories. This is necessary for the stated purpose, but incorrect paths could overwrite project files or process unintended PDFs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read and write targets are clearly documented in command examples and output descriptions. The behavior is legitimate but should be disclosed because it creates persistent files.

low_findings.title: Static Path Traversal Alerts Are Relative Path Examples
low_findings.description: The ../ sequences appear in documented command examples after changing into Calypso/tools. They point to expected project files and output directories; no evidence found of unvalidated path traversal logic in the skill file itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious sequences are visible, but their context is local relative addressing inside examples. I did not find evidence of malicious traversal or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance alerts do not correspond to cryptography, host enumeration, or malicious discovery behavior. They appear to be triggered by ordinary description and checklist text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain description or validation checklist language. I did not find cryptographic code, reconnaissance commands, network access, or prompt injection.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Invocation Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on python3 commands, including an inline python3 -c renderer. This pattern is acceptable for a PDF extraction skill when run in a trusted workspace with reviewed helper scripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is directly shown in the skill. Its purpose is clear, but it should remain visible as a marketplace risk factor.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Filesystem Path Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The command examples use relative parent paths for the PDF, analysis directory, mapping file, and output directory. These are expected project paths but require correct working directory control.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The paths are explicit in command examples. They are not malicious by themselves, but misuse could read or write unintended locations.

content.user_title: Extract PDF Pages into Traceable Artifacts
content.value_statement: PDF conversion loses layout context when text, images, and page numbers are handled separately. This skill creates persistent extraction artifacts that support layout-aware processing in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Guides validation of PDF paths, page ranges, output directories, and page mapping inputs.
Runs documented Python tools to create page mappings from PDF footers.
Extracts text spans with font, style, bounding box, color, and sequence metadata.
Renders selected PDF pages to high-resolution PNG images for visual reference.
Extracts embedded page images and writes image metadata for downstream workflows.
Defines quality checks for JSON validity, image readability, and extraction completeness.
limitations: Requires the referenced Calypso Python tools and their dependencies to exist in the workspace.
Does not include OCR; image-only pages are only marked for later handling.
Assumes the user supplies correct PDF paths, chapter numbers, and page ranges.
Runs local commands and writes files, so it should be used only in trusted workspaces.

use_cases.title: Prepare textbook pages for HTML conversion
use_cases.description: Create rich text, image, and mapping artifacts before generating layout-aware HTML pages.

use_cases.title: Audit PDF extraction quality
use_cases.description: Compare JSON metadata and rendered page images to verify that extraction captured the intended content.

use_cases.title: Build reusable document processing pipelines
use_cases.description: Save deterministic extraction outputs that later skills can reuse without reprocessing the source PDF.

prompt_templates.title: Extract one PDF page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pdf-page-extract to extract chapter 2, PDF index 15, from my Calypso PDF. Validate the output JSON and PNG artifacts.

prompt_templates.title: Extract a page range
prompt_templates.prompt: Run pdf-page-extract for chapter 3 from PDF index 29 through 42. Create page mapping, rich extraction JSON, rendered PNGs, and embedded image metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Check an existing extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the artifacts created by pdf-page-extract for chapter 2. Confirm that text spans, page mappings, and rendered PNG files are complete and readable.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare downstream HTML generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pdf-page-extract to regenerate chapter 4 artifacts, then summarize which files are ready for the next HTML generation skill and which pages need manual review.

output_examples.input: Extract chapter 2 pages from PDF indices 15 to 28.
output: Page mapping file created or updated for the selected range.
Rich extraction data saved for each page with text spans and layout metadata.
Rendered PNG page images saved under the chapter page artifact folders.
The rich extraction file parses successfully and contains text span data.
The rendered page PNG is present and readable.
The page mapping entry is consistent with the selected PDF index.

output_examples.input: Extract embedded images for a selected page.
output: Embedded page images are saved to the chapter image output directory.
Image metadata records are created for downstream processing.
Pages without embedded images are handled without stopping the workflow.

best_practices: Review the referenced Python scripts before running the skill in a new repository.
Use explicit PDF paths, page ranges, and output directories for each extraction run.
Validate JSON and PNG artifacts before handing results to later conversion steps.
anti_patterns: Do not run the skill against untrusted repository scripts without review.
Do not reuse stale page mapping files after changing the source PDF or page range.
Do not assume image-only pages contain extracted text without OCR validation.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates page mapping data, rich text extraction JSON, rendered PNG page images, and embedded image metadata.

faq.question: Does this skill perform OCR?
faq.answer: No. It can identify image-only pages, but OCR must be handled by a later workflow.

faq.question: Which tools does it depend on?
faq.answer: It expects local Python tools that use PyMuPDF and pdfplumber for PDF analysis and rendering.

faq.question: Can it process multiple pages?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented commands support a start and end PDF index for page range extraction.

faq.question: Why are there security warnings?
faq.answer: The skill runs local Python commands and writes files. Use it only in trusted workspaces with reviewed scripts.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning. The risky patterns are expected workflow behavior, not confirmed malicious activity.
